title: Policy Decision (PDP verdict, AuthZEN-aligned)
description: A verdict issued by a Policy Decision Point (PDP) in response to a subject/resource/action/context request, to be enforced by a Policy Enforcement Point (PEP). Splitting the governance gate into a PDP (which decides) and a PEP (which enforces) is SDE-0 conformance requirement 4: the decision and its enforcement live outside the AI model. The request/decision/obligations shape follows the OpenID AuthZEN Authorization API 1.0, an OpenID Final Specification dated 2026-01-11 and approved by the OpenID Foundation membership on 2026-01-12, https://openid.net/specs/authorization-api-1_0.html. The PDP/PEP split itself originates in the ABAC/XACML tradition; AuthZEN standardizes the API between them. A decision authorizes one action against one resource; it does not certify real-world outcome, which is reconciled later (schemas/execution-receipt.schema.json). Grounding for keeping this authorization layer deterministic: IMF Note 2026/004 'How Agentic AI Will Reshape Payments' (2026-04-22) places authorization/control in a strictly deterministic layer separate from probabilistic intent/orchestration, https://www.imf.org/en/publications/imf-notes/issues/2026/04/22/how-agentic-ai-will-reshape-payments-575560.

id.description: Stable, globally unique identifier for this decision. Referenced by execution-receipt.decisionRef.

request.description: The access-evaluation request the PDP ruled on, in AuthZEN's subject/resource/action/context tuple form.

subject.description: The requesting entity, typically the agent identity from action-mandate.agentId.

resource.description: The resource or target the action would be applied to.

action.description: The action type requested (must match a mandate allowedAction to be permitted).

context.description: Additional attributes the PDP considered (for example current budget spend, twin-state confidence, time of day, freshness of inputs).

decision.description: The PDP verdict. 'permit' authorizes the single requested action subject to any obligations; 'deny' forbids it. Anything other than an explicit permit MUST be enforced as a deny.

obligations.description: Actions the PEP MUST perform as a condition of enforcing a permit (for example 'log-to-audit', 'require-human-approval', 'attach-receipt'). AuthZEN-style obligations.

reasons.description: Human-readable justifications for the verdict, especially for a deny (for example 'budget cap exceeded', 'action not in mandate scope', 'mandate revoked').

policyRefs.description: Identifiers of the policies or rules applied, supporting policy-to-code traceability (code complements, does not replace, organizational responsibility).

mandateRef.description: Identifier of the action-mandate (action-mandate.id) this decision was evaluated against.

decidedAt.description: RFC 3339 instant the decision was rendered.
